agents.name: backend
agents.description: Executes backend plans — API, database, server-side logic

agents.name: devops
agents.description: Manages infrastructure-as-code, CI/CD pipelines, containers, and deployment automation

agents.name: frontend
agents.description: Executes frontend plans — UI components, state management, styling

agents.name: fullstack
agents.description: Implements full-stack features end-to-end — UI, API routes, database, auth, tests

agents.name: security
agents.description: Audits code for vulnerabilities, reviews auth patterns, scans dependencies, enforces security standards

agents.name: tech-lead
agents.description: Architects the system, writes plans, reviews agent output — never writes application code

skills.name: api-design-standards
skills.description: REST API design conventions — URLs, methods, errors, pagination, versioning

scenarios: Designing or reviewing REST/GraphQL API endpoints
Defining request/response schemas or versioning strategy

skills.name: auth-patterns
skills.description: Authentication and authorization patterns — flows, tokens, passwords, sessions, access control

scenarios: Implementing or reviewing authentication or authorization logic
Working with session management, tokens, or access control

skills.name: cli-conventions
skills.description: CLI design conventions — structure, flags, output, exit codes, configuration

scenarios: Building or reviewing CLI commands, flags, or argument parsing
Designing command-line user experience

skills.name: coding-standards
skills.description: Language-specific coding standards — formatting, linting, patterns

scenarios: Writing or reviewing source code in any language
Applying formatting, linting, or naming conventions

skills.name: container-standards
skills.description: Container and orchestration standards — Docker multi-stage builds, image security, Kubernetes manifests

scenarios: Writing or reviewing Dockerfiles, docker-compose, or Kubernetes manifests
Configuring container builds, images, or orchestration

skills.name: database-conventions
skills.description: SQL naming, migration patterns, schema design rules

scenarios: Writing or reviewing SQL migrations or database schemas
Designing data models or query patterns

skills.name: design-guide
skills.description: Bonsai CLI design system — palette, spacing, panels, errors, command flow

scenarios: Modifying TUI styles, panels, display helpers, or color definitions
Adding or changing CLI output formatting in any command

skills.name: dispatch
skills.description: How to dispatch work to code agents — triage rules, prompt structure, iteration tracking

scenarios: Dispatching work to a code agent via worktree
Writing an agent dispatch prompt with plan steps

skills.name: iac-conventions
skills.description: Infrastructure-as-code conventions — Terraform naming, state management, modules, tagging

scenarios: Writing or reviewing Terraform, CloudFormation, or infrastructure-as-code
Managing cloud resources or infrastructure configuration

skills.name: issue-classification
skills.description: Issue types, importance levels, domain labels, and classification heuristics

scenarios: Classifying or triaging a new issue or bug report
Determining issue type, importance, and domain labels

skills.name: mobile-patterns
skills.description: Mobile development patterns — state management, offline-first, navigation, performance

scenarios: Developing or reviewing iOS or Android application code
Working with mobile-specific patterns, navigation, or platform APIs

skills.name: planning-template
skills.description: Plan format, tier rules, and template for writing implementation plans

scenarios: Writing a new implementation plan
Structuring a plan with tier rules and verification steps

skills.name: pr-creation
skills.description: How to create well-structured pull requests — branch naming, title conventions, body template, draft workflow

scenarios: Creating a pull request with proper conventions
Setting up branch naming, PR title, and body template

skills.name: review-checklist
skills.description: Structured code review checklist — correctness, security, performance, maintainability

scenarios: Performing a structured code review
Checking correctness, security, performance, and maintainability

skills.name: test-strategy
skills.description: Test architecture — which tests to write, when, and why; pyramid, coverage, anti-patterns

scenarios: Defining the overall testing approach for a feature or module
Deciding which test types to use and what to prioritize

skills.name: testing
skills.description: Testing conventions — frameworks, patterns, coverage requirements

scenarios: Writing or reviewing unit tests, integration tests, or test fixtures
Evaluating test coverage or test quality

skills.name: workspace-guide
skills.description: Bonsai workspace operational guide — how to use workflows, skills, routines, sensors, and the Playbook effectively

scenarios: Learning how this agent workspace is organized
Understanding workspace conventions and file purposes

workflows.name: api-development
workflows.description: Spec-first API development — define contract, generate scaffolding, implement, test, document

scenarios: Building a new API endpoint or service from requirements
Implementing CRUD operations, middleware, or API integrations

examples.action: Load api-development workflow, design endpoints, implement handlers

workflows.name: code-review
workflows.description: Review agent output against the plan — correctness, standards, security

scenarios: Reviewing agent output against the plan for correctness and standards
Checking implementation changes before merging

examples.prompt: Review the changes on the feature branch
examples.action: Load code-review workflow, diff branch against main, check plan compliance

workflows.name: issue-to-implementation
workflows.description: End-to-end autonomous workflow — issue intake, analysis, research, planning, agent dispatch, review loop, logging, audit, and close

scenarios: Taking an issue from intake through to shipped code
Running the full autonomous implementation workflow

examples.prompt: Pick up issue #15 and ship it
examples.action: Load issue-to-implementation workflow, analyze issue, plan, dispatch, review, merge

workflows.name: plan-execution
workflows.description: Execute an assigned plan step by step — implement, test, report

scenarios: Executing an approved implementation plan step by step
Dispatching agents and tracking plan completion

examples.action: Load plan-execution workflow, read plan, dispatch agents, track progress

workflows.name: planning
workflows.description: End-to-end planning process — from request to dispatch-ready plan

scenarios: Starting end-to-end planning for a new feature or task
Translating requirements into a structured implementation plan

examples.action: Load planning workflow, analyze requirements, write plan to Plans/Active/

workflows.name: pr-review
workflows.description: Review a pull request — context, scope, correctness, security, performance, standards

scenarios: Reviewing a pull request for correctness, security, and standards
Evaluating PR scope, changes, and test coverage

examples.action: Load pr-review workflow, fetch PR details, review against checklist

workflows.name: reporting
workflows.description: Completion report format — what was done, tested, issues encountered

scenarios: Generating a structured completion or status report
Documenting project progress, findings, or audit results

examples.prompt: Write the completion report for this task
examples.action: Load reporting workflow, compile findings, write to Reports/Pending/

workflows.name: security-audit
workflows.description: Security audit — secrets scan, dependency audit, SAST, config review, access control, infrastructure

scenarios: Running a security audit on the codebase or recent changes
Checking for secrets, vulnerable dependencies, or unsafe patterns

examples.prompt: Check the project for security issues
examples.action: Load security-audit workflow, scan secrets, audit deps, review config

workflows.name: session-logging
workflows.description: End-of-session log — what was done, decisions made, open items

scenarios: Writing an end-of-session log entry
Recording decisions made and open items from the current session

examples.action: Load session-logging workflow, compile session summary, append to logs

workflows.name: test-plan
workflows.description: Design a structured test plan for a feature — scope, prioritize, allocate test types

scenarios: Designing a structured test plan for a feature or module
Deciding test scope, priorities, and test type allocation

examples.prompt: Plan the tests for the new API endpoints
examples.action: Load test-plan workflow, analyze feature, design test coverage strategy

protocols.name: memory
protocols.description: How to read, write, and maintain working memory between sessions

protocols.name: scope-boundaries
protocols.description: What you own, what you never touch, workspace boundaries

protocols.name: security
protocols.description: Security enforcement — hard stops and hard enforcers

protocols.name: session-start
protocols.description: Ordered startup sequence — what to read and check every session

sensors.name: agent-review
sensors.description: Outputs a review checklist after a dispatched agent completes work

sensors.name: api-security-check
sensors.description: Detects security anti-patterns in code changes — SQL injection, hardcoded secrets, eval, CORS wildcards

sensors.name: compact-recovery
sensors.description: Re-injects minimal context after /compact (Quick Triggers + Work State only)

sensors.name: context-guard
sensors.description: Injects context-aware behavioral constraints and detects session wrap-up trigger words before each prompt

sensors.name: dispatch-guard
sensors.description: Validates code agent dispatches — requires worktree isolation, plan reference, and plan existence before execution

sensors.name: iac-safety-guard
sensors.description: Blocks dangerous infrastructure commands — terraform destroy, kubectl delete namespace, unsafe docker ops

sensors.name: routine-check
sensors.description: Checks routine dashboard at session start and flags overdue maintenance routines

sensors.name: scope-guard-commands
sensors.description: Blocks agent from running application execution commands (tests, builds, servers)

sensors.name: scope-guard-files
sensors.description: Blocks agent from editing files outside its workspace

sensors.name: session-context
sensors.description: Injects core identity, memory, protocols, and project status at session start

sensors.name: status-bar
sensors.description: Persistent status line showing context usage, session health, and git state after every response

sensors.name: subagent-stop-review
sensors.description: Outputs a structured review checklist when a dispatched agent finishes work

sensors.name: test-integrity-guard
sensors.description: Detects test quality issues — removed assertions, .skip/.only, empty test bodies, debug artifacts

routines.name: backlog-hygiene
routines.description: Review and maintain Playbook/Backlog.md — flag stale items, escalate P0s, cross-reference with Status and Roadmap

routines.name: dependency-audit
routines.description: Scan dependencies for known vulnerabilities and unmaintained packages

routines.name: doc-freshness-check
routines.description: Detect documentation drift — docs that are stale relative to recent code changes

routines.name: infra-drift-check
routines.description: Detect infrastructure drift — compare declared IaC state against actual cloud resources

routines.name: memory-consolidation
routines.description: Bridge Claude Code auto-memory into agent/Core/memory.md — validate facts, clean redundant entries

routines.name: roadmap-accuracy
routines.description: Ensure Playbook/Roadmap.md reflects what's actually built and what's actually next

routines.name: status-hygiene
routines.description: Keep Playbook/Status.md clean — archive old Done items, validate Pending items

routines.name: vulnerability-scan
routines.description: SAST scan, secrets scan, and dependency audit — flag new, resolved, and persistent vulnerabilities
